items.prompt: exposure: scripts/.discord_webhook.txt in Scheduler is git-tracked and contains a live discord.com/api/webhooks URL (first committed in cf05134, feat(scheduler): v0.2.0). Mitigate: (1) add `scripts/.discord_webhook.txt` to .gitignore; (2) `git rm --cached scripts/.discord_webhook.txt`; (3) commit the redaction; (4) rotate the webhook on Discord (the leaked URL is permanently in git history and trivially recoverable). History scrub via `git filter-repo --path scripts/.discord_webhook.txt --invert-paths` is a separate manual call after rotation. Compare YaC + YaApothecary, which already use `.discord_webhook.txt.example` + gitignored real file.
items.notes: Surfaced by working-tree-scan-weekly 2026-05-29. Same secret-file pattern as the YaB .finances/ exposure earlier in the cycle.
items.note: Repo-side mitigation verified done 2026-06-10: scripts/.discord_webhook.txt no longer tracked at HEAD and .gitignore covers it (line 53). Residual: the URL lives in git history (cf05134), so the webhook itself needs rotation in Discord (user action), then paste the new URL into scripts/.discord_webhook.txt. History scrub optional if repo stays private.

items.prompt: YaC has one stash entry: `stash@{0}: On main: scraper WIP`. Review and either apply (then ship or re-stash with clearer label), or drop. `git -C X:\YesAndChains stash show -p stash@{0}` to inspect; `git stash apply stash@{0}` or `git stash drop stash@{0}` to act. Stashes live in the reflog and can be reconstructed for 90 days, so the safer move is `apply` and decide in-tree, not blind drop.

items.title: After 0.34.2 ships: repoint live tool references to tools/_archived/ (CONTEXT.md:210 scrape_pipeline.py; BACKLOG.md:395/496/1051 seed_courses.py + scrape_pipeline.py). Leave historical changelog rows untouched.
items.notes: Dependent on yac-mnew-ship-0.34.2 committing the archive move first. Do NOT rewrite historical CONTEXT.md changelog entries that name these tools. | 2026-06-04 17:08Z drain: blocking dep yac-mnew-ship-0.34.2 now COMPLETED (0.34.2 shipped, archive move committed), so unblocked. NOT auto-drained: not a mechanical path swap. Stale line numbers (CONTEXT.md:210->235, BACKLOG.md:395/496/1051->248/398/499/1054). CONTEXT.md:235 names scrape_pipeline.py inside a LIVE scraper-workflow description while the tool now lives in tools/_archived/ and is absent from the CLAUDE.md 'six active scripts' list -- that's a live-vs-dead-workflow judgment, not a repoint. BACKLOG.md 248/398/499 + CONTEXT.md:9 are historical/forward-fix rows -> leave untouched per item spec. 218KB CONTEXT.md = FUSE risk; needs a focused session. Left pending. | 2026-06-05 13:10Z drain: grounded the remaining scope. CONTEXT.md refs are all historical EXCEPT line ~236 (the 'Course Data Gathering -- Sonnet' manual scraper-session description, which names scrape_pipeline.py as the live tool). That workflow was superseded by ADR 0021 (local cron via cron_course_refresh.py + run-course-refresh.ps1 / Windows Task Scheduler); CLAUDE.md's six-active-scripts list does not mention scrape_pipeline.py at all. BACKLOG 30.2 ('Update tools/scrape_pipeline.py to write to per-state shard') is likely obsolete for the same reason. So this is NOT a path repoint -- it is a confirm-and-retire of a dead manual workflow in a 336KB CONTEXT.md over FUSE. Recommend: Nick confirms the manual scraper session is retired, then rewrite CONTEXT.md:236 to describe the cron pipeline and mark BACKLOG 30.2 done/obsolete in a focused session. Left pending.
items.note: Drain 2026-06-07: original line numbers (CONTEXT.md:210, BACKLOG.md:395/496/1051) are stale (added pre-0.34.2, repo now 0.50.0). Re-survey done. The one true live/operational stale ref is the Background-processes scraper description at CONTEXT.md:256 (and the surrounding 252-258 block), which still describes a separate "Course Data Gathering - Sonnet" session calling tools/scrape_pipeline.py. CLAUDE.md + ADR 0021 indicate ingestion moved to cron_course_refresh.py -> course_parser.py via Windows Task Scheduler. NEEDS NICK: confirm the manual scraper session is retired, then rewrite CONTEXT.md 252-258 to the cron model. Not a mechanical repoint; left for attended review.

items.title: Portfolio-wide unkillable .git/index.lock blocks every git write
items.prompt: All six repos carry a stale .git/index.lock that cannot be removed from the agent sandbox (rm returns 'Operation not permitted' across the FUSE permission boundary). Timestamps differ per repo (YaB/YaA 12:22, Scheduler 13:08, BR 18:31, HBH/YaC 18:57) so it is not one process - it is the recurring FUSE lock-survival hazard now hitting the whole portfolio at once. EFFECT: no commit/add/push/release works from any session; the audit-to-commit loop, BR's staged v0.57.x release, and the YaB doc-batch drain are all wedged behind it. FIX (Windows side, Nick): run the git-unstick skill or unstick-git.ps1 against each repo (it force-removes the lock from the Windows side), then the queued doc-commit + release items become drainable again. Until then every commit-bearing queue item is structurally blocked.
items.blockedReason: stale .git/index.lock unremovable from sandbox on all 6 repos; needs Windows-side git-unstick / unstick-git.ps1
items.notes: Surfaced by loop-tick-frequent 2026-06-05T21:10. Generalizes the YaB-only block (yab-doc-batch-blocked-lock-and-voice-2026-06-05) to the full portfolio. Supersedes per-repo lock diagnosis - clear all six in one git-unstick pass.

items.title: YaB stalled-tree close-out: clean .bak, set EOL policy, fix BOM, ship in two commits
items.prompt: YaB's 2026-06-06 bar-raise verdict is 'stalled' (fifth consecutive). Head is e6cac08 (2026-05-29), eight days cold, and the working tree is now 26+ uncommitted items that are no longer doc-only, so the old 'one doc-only commit closes it' plan is dead. Close it out in this order: (1) git-unstick the unremovable .git/index.lock from the Windows side first (sandbox rm returns Operation-not-permitted); (2) delete scripts/discord-notify.ps1.bak and scripts/uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1.bak, or add '*.bak' to .gitignore; (3) fix the UTF-8 BOM (ef bb bf) the overnight drift pass stamped onto scripts/discord-notify.ps1 and scripts/uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1 (release.ps1 does not carry a BOM); (4) add a .gitattributes with '* text=auto' and renormalize once so the CRLF flip stops recurring every session; (5) fix em-dash voice violations in docs/DECISIONS.md (D-001..D-007) and the CANONICAL_AUDIT reports before commit (run solo-dev-voice-audit), and decide whether the auto-generated audit reports get the same scrub or are exempted; (6) ship in two coherent commits through scripts/release.ps1: 'docs(yab): land F1-F11 canonical batch + 05-29..06-06 audit/bar-raise reports' and 'chore(yab): cross-project cwd guard (CLAUDE.md command rule + script self-location)'.
items.blockedReason: unremovable .git/index.lock (needs Windows-side git-unstick) + em-dash voice fixes; not auto-drainable from the sandbox

Related: wts-2026-06-05-yab-stale-working-tree (P2), wts-2026-06-05-portfolio-wide-index-lock (P0).

items.title: Reconcile POLISH_LOG.md v0.11.0 'shipped' claim with reality (nothing is at 0.11.0)
items.prompt: docs/POLISH_LOG.md line 3 says '...shipped together. All items applied; release at v0.11.0' and the Release section (line 148) declares v0.11.0 — but no version marker agrees: all four package.json read 0.10.0, the App.tsx pill (line 117) is v0.10.0, CHANGELOG.md has no 0.11.0 entry, DESIGN.md does not mention v0.11.0, and HEAD is e6cac08. The polish CONTENT is genuinely applied (format.ts has all 7 claimed helpers; index.css has the claimed utilities; the components exist) — only the RELEASE is fictional. Resolve one of two ways: EITHER actually ship v0.11.0 via scripts/release.ps1 (bump all 4 package.json + the App.tsx pill + DESIGN.md header 'Version:' + DESIGN §10 milestone row + a CHANGELOG v0.11.0 entry), OR downgrade POLISH_LOG.md language from 'shipped/release at v0.11.0' to 'applied to working tree, pending v0.11.0 release' so an in-repo doc stops asserting a release state that doesn't exist. Tie to yab-index-lock-cleared-commit-now (the release is now unblocked).

Verified bar-raise 06-11 spec drifts (sec2 SVG claim, sec8 PNG-only lock) already closed at HEAD by v0.17.0; those report actions are stale, report predates the 15:51Z releases. Remaining gate path: preset-custom-items decision (Nick; the uncommitted state.js comment edit presupposes one side of it, hold that line until decided), release double-fire fix (confirmed real: v0.17.1/v0.17.2 identical messages), CLAUDE.md export-png row, then commit in-flight spec edits + final patch release. attempts NOT incremented: slice succeeded, no failure; do not auto-block. Verdict note per rule 5: CHANGELOG truncation was the only breakage signal on YaA and is fixed; next review should clear YaA toward working/in-progress.

items.prompt: BR M1 survival loop: Oil-zero mission-fail wiring now EXISTS in the working tree (gameplay.gd + command_post.gd, uncommitted since 2026-06-08, 10 modified + 3 untracked files). Nick action: commit it with its own changelog entry via cd X:\BrackishRising; .\scripts\release.ps1, then wire the remaining loop pieces (wave cadence check, fog of war, win state). See docs/BAR_RAISE-2026-06-11.md + docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-11.md.

Committed-ready. attempts counter reflects deliberate slicing, not failure - do not auto-block. Remaining slices: wave cadence (needs Nick: 180s-cadence row contradicts mission.gd target_day design), SP wave-tier scaling, convoy, FoW wiring verify. See result file. | 2026-06-11 overnight drain slice 2: FoW wiring VERIFIED shipped (bk-fow P0/MVP row). Instantiation per mission under iso_grid, continuous-position stamping per v0.74.37+v0.35.4, per-Node enemy fade + re-sleep, minimap state render all confirmed wired. Zero code changes. Dormant divergence flagged: pool path never writes EnemyData.modulate_a from fog (all use_multimesh flags false, so inert; wire before enabling pool). bk-fow markable done in GDD UI (status is localStorage). attempts NOT incremented: slices completed successfully, no failures; do not auto-block. Remaining slices: SP wave-tier scaling, convoy, wave cadence (needs Nick). See slice2 result file.

items.prompt: source/ui/minimap/minimap.gd declares TILE_W: float = 64.0 and TILE_H: float = 32.0 (pre-v0.76.0 values, 2x current 32/16). It evades scripts/check_constants_mirrors.py because the guard regex only matches the int-typed consts. Two things to settle: (1) determine whether minimap intentionally renders at its own cell scale or whether this is genuine drift that halves-behind the v0.76.0 rework (if drift, fix the values and check minimap rendering); (2) extend check_constants_mirrors.py to also match float-typed const declarations, with an allowlist entry if minimap is intentional. Code change, so not auto_safe.

Full snapshot refresh at current version.
Stale .git/index.lock sits in X:\BrackishRising; the sandbox mount denies unlink. Clear Windows-side (release.ps1 does it as step one, or: cd X:\BrackishRising; Remove-Item .git\index.lock).

YaC CLAUDE.md refresh: (1) CONTEXT.md size stated as 218KB at lines 15 and 81; actual ~388KB. (2) tools/ described as eleven active scripts; now twelve, audit_live_pin_data.py missing from the table. (3) index.html ~93KB is now ~105KB; app.js ~1MB is now ~1.4MB. Numbers only, keep guidance intact.

Host-side: cd X:\YesAndEverything; .\unstick-git.ps1 (clears the stale zero-byte .git/index.lock from 2026-06-10); then verify git status shows only .next-loop.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-YAE-2026-06-11.md, and queue files as modified (the 18 restored files should be clean vs HEAD); then .\scripts\release.ps1 to commit. If any of the 18 restored files still show modified beyond line-ending normalization, stop and inspect before committing.

Find the writer that tail-truncated 19 YaE files (mtimes Jun 8-11; scripts/collect-usage.ps1 hit at Jun 11 02:01 over uncommitted edits, now lost): all cut mid-token with no trailing newline; work/index.html null-padded to 17,094 B; apothecary/index.html corrupted at byte level. Pattern matches the known FUSE mid-write truncation hazard but the breadth points at one bad session, likely a scheduled loop run between Jun 8 and Jun 10 20:02 (index.lock timestamp). Check .queue-breadcrumbs.log, .work-queue-results/, and session transcripts for writes to these paths without atomic-write readback; harden the offending script to safe_write-style writes.
